text.title: testing-with-assistive-technology Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team claims 'WCAG 2.1 AA' from axe-clean dashboard; real screen-reader user cannot complete onboarding.
antipattern.root-cause: Manual + AT-user sweep skipped; automated tools cannot detect context-dependent failures.
antipattern.fix: Mandate the three-tier config; reject conformance claims without manual + AT-user evidence.

antipattern.symptom: Per-release a11y scan finds 80+ regressions; release slips by a week.
antipattern.fix: Run automated scan on every PR; fail build on new violations.

antipattern.symptom: AT-user session deferred quarter after quarter; lawsuit reveals issues invisible in manual sweep.
antipattern.fix: Budget AT-user sessions as a fixed quarterly cost; track in OKRs.

antipattern.symptom: Bug 'screen reader is bad here' filed; engineering cannot reproduce.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped SC reference + repro snippet.
antipattern.fix: Mandate bug-report template with WCAG SC + repro + APG pattern + scope.
